title: Event refactor: Profile-based attendance with multi-profile UI
description: Refactor event attendance from user_id to profile_id across all layers. Update domain EventRepository interface (SignUp, Withdraw, GetAttendees accept profile_id). Update mock EventRepository. Update migration (already covered by issue scout-app-9zn). Refactor event detail view: replace single sign-up button with a list of manageable profiles (user's own Profile + any linked youth Profiles). Each profile gets its own Sign Up / Withdraw button. Attendee list shows profile names (first+last) instead of emails. Update EventListItem attendee count to reflect profile count. Update EventHandler and all tests.

title: Registration flow: OTP verification and password creation
description: Build the /register → /register/verify → /register/complete flow. GET/POST /register: email input, look up Profile by email (reject if not found or already linked), generate 6-digit OTP, store in otp_codes, send via EmailService. GET/POST /register/verify: OTP input, validate code (not expired, not used), mark used. GET/POST /register/complete: password creation form, create User with bcrypt hash, link to Profile, set up session cookie, redirect to /events. All views are HTMX Go templates. Update login flow to find Profile by email → get linked User → verify password.

title: Migration: profiles, otp_codes, parent_youth_links, scoutbook_sessions tables + attendance refactor
description: Create new SQL migration that adds profiles, otp_codes, parent_youth_links, scoutbook_sessions tables. Migrate event_attendees from user_id to profile_id. Migrate event_attendee_responsibilities from user_id to profile_id. User table loses email column. Add unique index on profiles.bsa_id.

title: Domain model: Profile, OTPCode, ParentYouthLink, ScoutbookSession
description: Add Profile struct, OTPCode, ParentYouthLink, ScoutbookSession types to internal/domain/models.go. Add ProfileRepository, OTPRepository, ParentYouthLinkRepository, ScoutbookSessionRepository interfaces to internal/domain/repositories.go. Implement mock repositories. Add OTP generation/validation domain logic with expiry and used status. This is the foundation all other features depend on.

title: Detailed sync report in response
description:
After sync finishes, include a detailed per-profile report in the response (not saved to DB) so admins can review exactly what changed.

Each profile entry should show:
- memberId, name, status (created/updated/skipped/deactivated)
- For updated profiles: which fields changed (old → new)
- Role changes: which roles were added/removed

The report is returned inline in the sync HTTP response. No database storage.
acceptance_criteria: Sync response includes per-profile report|Each profile shows its sync status|Updated profiles show old→new for each changed field|Role additions and removals are listed per profile|No data is persisted to database|All existing tests pass
notes:
Design finalized via grill-with-docs session.

Data model:
- ProfileSnapshot — captures all sync-relevant fields (same fields as profile minus ID/CreatedAt/UpdatedAt)
- ProfileReport — one per changed profile with Status (created/updated/deactivated), Old/New snapshots, role changes
- Result.Profiles — only changed profiles (no skipped entries)
- Aggregate counters computed from Profiles slice at end of Sync() — single source of truth

Revertability: ProfileSnapshot includes BSAID, UserID so future revert task can post Old snapshot back.

Presentation: Template renders per-profile diff with old→new field changes and role additions/removals. Revert UI (separate issue) would remove the row from the report on success.

title: Sync role reconciliation for linked users
description:
After sync updates a profile's positions, if the profile has a linked user, reconcile their position-based roles:

1. Parse the profile's Positions field (comma-separated) into individual position names
2. For each position, look up the matching role by name
3. If a position doesn't match any existing role, auto-create it (with zero permissions) so the sync never fails on unknown positions
4. Add any position-based roles the user doesn't already have
5. Remove any position-based roles the user no longer holds (compared against current positions)
6. Never touch the 'parent' or 'admin' roles

The Sync Result struct should report how many roles were added and removed.
acceptance_criteria: After profile positions are updated during sync, if profile has a linked user, reconcile user's position-based roles|Roles matching current positions are added|Position-based roles no longer held are removed|parent and admin roles are never touched|No-op if profile has no linked user|Sync Result struct reports roles added/removed

title: Seed all Scoutbook positions as roles
acceptance_criteria: All predefined Scoutbook position strings exist as roles in the roles table|scoutmaster renamed to Scoutmaster, asst_scoutmaster renamed to Assistant Scoutmaster, scout renamed to Scouts BSA|Existing role-permission mappings preserved for Scoutmaster, Assistant Scoutmaster, Scouts BSA, admin, parent|New roles (e.g. Scribe, Patrol Leader, etc.) have zero permissions|Migration is idempotent (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING)|SeedRoles() in auth.go kept in sync

title: Need a way to bootstrap an admin account the first time the service is run
description:
There should be a mechanism to create the initial admin account when the service starts for the first time (e.g., seed, first-run setup, or env-based credentials).

Idea: Add an onboarding flow when the app starts with zero profiles. The flow:

1. Prompt for first name, last name, email, BSA ID, unit type, unit number, Scoutbook organization GUID, and IANA timezone (e.g. America/New_York).
2. Seed the first Profile with that information.
3. Prompt to create a password (for local auth).
4. Redirect to login.
5. The first profile is granted the admin role.
6. On the first sync from Scoutbook, the profile with the matching BSA ID supersedes the seeded local profile (merged/overwritten).
acceptance_criteria: Onboarding flow implemented: app_config table, multi-step UI at /onboard/*, middleware guards, Profile/User/role creation, app config persistence, sync handler reads org GUID from DB, ScoutbookOrgGUID env var optional, hardcoded seed removed from production path.

title: Claim youth flow: BSA ID linking with admin approval
description: Build (/claim-youth) page for registered parents. Form that takes a youth's BSA ID. Validates the BSA ID matches an existing Profile with member_type=youth and no existing linked User. Creates a ParentYouthLink record with status=pending. Admin approves or rejects on the /admin/links page. Once approved, the parent's event detail page shows the youth's name with individual sign-up/withdraw buttons.

title: Email service: SMTP integration for OTP delivery
description: Define EmailService interface in domain with SendOTP(recipient, code) method. Implement SMTP-based email sender using net/smtp with env vars SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS, SMTP_FROM. Wire into the app config in main.go. Add mock email sender for tests that captures sent emails in memory.

comments.author: Quincy Bowers
comments.text: Implemented email service infrastructure: Service interface in internal/domain/email/, SMTP sender with net/smtp in internal/email/smtp.go, embedded text/template system in internal/email/templates.go with OTP verification template. Config is wired in main.go. Mock email service added to storage/mock. Registration handlers (endpoints) are still a separate task.

title: Admin page: Parent-youth link approval queue
description: Build admin-only page (/admin/links) that shows a queue of pending parent-youth link requests. Each row shows requesting parent name, youth name, youth BSA ID, and requested timestamp. Approve and Reject buttons. On approval: update link status to approved, establish the parent-youth relationship. On rejection: update to rejected. After approval, parent can act on behalf of that youth in event sign-ups.

title: Admin page: Scoutbook sync with token paste
description: Build admin-only page (/admin/sync) where admin can paste their LOGIN_DATA JSON from advancements.scouting.org localStorage. Decode and store the JWT in scoutbook_sessions table. Sync button triggers the SyncService with a spinner/progress indicator. Show sync results: profiles created, updated, marked inactive, and any errors.

title: Scoutbook sync engine: API client and sync logic
description: Build the Scoutbook API client in a new internal/scoutbook/ package. Implement a ScoutbookClient that takes a JWT token and orgGuid, and can fetch orgAdults and orgYouths roster endpoints (POST with {includeRegistrationDetails:true, includeExpired:true}). Implement dedup by memberId (BSA ID). Implement concurrent personprofile fetching for email/phone/birthdate. Implement the SyncService in domain that orchestrates: fetch → dedup → fetch profiles → upsert local Profiles → mark missing as inactive. Email sync rule: Scoutbook overwrites local if different; local preserved if Scoutbook has none.

title: Event delete with custom HTMX confirmation modal
description: Implement event deletion with a custom HTMX-driven confirmation modal. On the event detail page, a Delete button (visible with event:create) triggers an HTMX GET that fetches a confirmation partial into a fixed overlay modal. The modal contains a form that submits via hx-delete to /events/{id}/delete. On success, HX-Redirect to /events with ?deleted=1. On error, swap error message into the modal.
acceptance_criteria: Delete button visible on event detail page for users with event:create|Clicking Delete opens a custom modal overlay (not browser confirm())|Modal is fetched via HTMX GET partial|Modal contains Cancel button and Confirm Delete button|Confirm Delete submits via hx-delete to /events/{id}/delete|On success, redirects to /events with ?deleted=1 flash|On error, error message shown in modal|Deleting with attendees works (cascade)|Deleting past events works|Route is gated by event:create permission

title: Event edit form using shared template
description: Implement the event editing flow using the same event_form.html template as create. GET /events/{id}/edit pre-populates the form with existing event data. POST /events/{id}/edit validates and updates via the repository. Add an Edit button on the event detail page visible to users with event:create permission. On success, redirect to the event detail page with ?updated=1 flash message.
acceptance_criteria: GET /events/{id}/edit renders the form pre-filled with existing event data|POST /events/{id}/edit validates required fields|On success, redirects to /events/{id} with ?updated=1|On validation error, re-renders form with error message|Edit button visible on event detail page for users with event:create|Route is gated by event:create permission|Same event_form.html template is shared with create|UpdatedAt timestamp is refreshed on successful update

title: Event create form with markdown split-view
description: Implement the event creation flow. Create event_form.html template with all fields (title, description, location, start_time, end_time, cost, type) and a split-view editor for the description (left: textarea, right: rendered markdown preview). Create GET /events/create to render the form and POST /events/create to handle submission. Add a Create Event button on the /events list page visible to users with event:create permission. On success, redirect to the event detail page with ?created=1 flash message.
acceptance_criteria: GET /events/create renders the event form with empty fields|POST /events/create validates all required fields (title, description, location, start_time, end_time, cost)|Split-view shows markdown textarea on left and live preview on right|Preview works via HTMX hx-trigger to /admin/markdown-preview|On success, redirects to /events/{id} with ?created=1|On validation error, re-renders form with error message|Create Event button visible on /events list for users with event:create|Route is gated by event:create permission

title: Add goldmark markdown rendering + markdown preview endpoint
description: Add github.com/yuin/goldmark dependency for server-side markdown rendering. Create a render helper function. Add a POST /admin/markdown-preview endpoint that accepts markdown text and returns rendered HTML for the live split-view preview on the event form.
acceptance_criteria: goldmark is added as a dependency|A Go render function converts markdown to HTML safely|POST /admin/markdown-preview accepts markdown text and returns HTML|Endpoint is gated by event:create permission|HTML is properly sanitized (no XSS from user markdown)|Tests cover the endpoint

title: Add Update/Delete to event repository + UpdatedAt field
description: Add Update and Delete methods to the event.Repository interface and implement them in both Postgres and mock implementations. Also add the missing UpdatedAt field to the Event struct.
acceptance_criteria: Repository interface has Update(ctx, *Event) error|Repository interface has Delete(ctx, id string) error|Postgres EventRepository implements Update and Delete|Mock EventRepository implements Update and Delete|Event struct has UpdatedAt field|UpdatedAt is populated on Update in Postgres|All repository tests pass

title: Registration assigns position-based roles
acceptance_criteria: At /register/complete, after assigning parent to adults, also assign roles matching profile's current Positions field|Same role reconciliation logic used by sync is applied here|Registration role assignment is idempotent (safe if profile has no positions)

title: Admin role assignment UI
acceptance_criteria: Admin-only route (e.g. /admin/roles/assign)|Lists users with their current non-position roles|Admin can grant/revoke the admin role|Sync never touches admin-assigned roles|HTMX-based, consistent with existing UI patterns

title: Admin permission management UI
acceptance_criteria: Admin-only route (e.g. /admin/permissions)|Lists all roles with checkboxes for each permission|Saving updates role_permissions at runtime|Permissions not in the seed list cannot be added via UI|Roles themselves cannot be created/deleted via UI|HTMX-based, consistent with existing UI patterns

title: Send email notification to admins on connection request
description:
When a parent submits a family connection request (POST /family-connections), send an email notification to all admin users so they know to review and approve/reject it on the /admin/links page.

Needs:
- Look up users with admin role
- Look up their profiles for email addresses
- Send email via existing email service with connection details (parent name, youth name, BSA ID)
- Handle errors gracefully (email failure should not block the connection request)

title: Allow more than one adult to be linked to the same youth profile
description: The current model appears to only support a single adult per youth profile. Need to support multiple adults (e.g., both parents) linked to the same youth.

title: Clarify status: active vs status: inactive for a profile
description: The meaning and behavior of active vs inactive profile status needs to be defined and documented. What does each status mean? What operations are allowed/blocked in each state?

title: Many profile fields are not being imported correctly from Scoutbook
description: Several profile fields are not being imported correctly from Scoutbook. Needs investigation to determine which fields are affected and fix the import mapping.

title: Implement Event Detail View
description: Implement the detail page for a single event, showing details and a list of attendees. Add self-sign-up and withdrawal logic with HTMX.

title: Implement Authentication/RBAC
description: Implement basic login screen and middleware to enforce role-based access control. Support multiple roles per user. Permissions should be mapped to roles.

title: Implement Event List View
description: Implement the chronological sliding window (10 events) with HTMX lazy loading for both future and past events. Sort events ASC.

title: Define Database Schema
description: Design and implement tables for Users, Roles, Permissions, Events, and Attendees in CockroachDB. Ensure database interactions are abstracted behind interfaces as per ADR 0001.

title: Set up Go environment
description: Initialize Git repository if not already done. Configure version control (Dolt, beads) and issue tracking.

title: Bundle HTMX library locally instead of loading from CDN
description:
Currently HTMX is loaded from unpkg CDN in base.html. Replace with a local copy served from the app's static assets.

Steps:
1. Download htmx.org@2.0.4 (or latest) to a static/ directory
2. Add a file server route in main.go for /static/ path
3. Update base.html to reference the local copy instead of CDN URL
4. Verify all HTMX-powered features still work (event list show-more, signup/withdraw, admin roster filter, etc.)
